pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh verification script
Task description and setup_task.sh verification script
Calculated using standard motor efficiency formula: (Output Power / Input Power) × 100. Conversion: 10 HP × 745.7 W/HP = 7457 W; (7457 W / 8500 W) × 100 = 87.729% ≈ 87.73%
Engineering standard tolerance for electrical efficiency calculations; verified as reasonable for motor efficiency calculations
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 10 cleanup) and export_result.sh (lines 12, 17-19 file operations)

privileged_info_summary: The motor efficiency analysis task uses realistic engineering parameters. A 10 HP (7457 W) motor with 8.5 kW input power yields an efficiency of 87.73%, calculated via the standard formula (Output / Input) × 100. This efficiency value is within the typical 80-95% range for electric motors. The task uses the real Electrical Calculations app (com.hsn.electricalcalculations, available on Google Play) which includes motor efficiency calculator functionality. The conversion factor 1 HP = 745.7 W is the standard mechanical horsepower definition. All metadata values are mathematically correct and consistent with the task scenario.
